lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Another desert preacher? Say your message plainly if you wish to enter.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Forty days, you say? Then go. The city should hear what troubles you.

lines.speaker: Banner
lines.text: The cloth bears lions and cedar. Empire stitched in bright dye.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Jonah entered the city and began to call out the warning entrusted to him.

lines.speaker: Herald
lines.text: You came through the gate with dust on your robe and judgment on your tongue. Say the warning again, loudly.

lines.speaker: Official
lines.text: The herald has carried your cry through the square. The king will hear it, but do not soften the word.

lines.speaker: King
lines.text: If this word is from the God of heaven, then let both man and beast be covered in humility. Let everyone turn from violence and cry mightily to God.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: From the throne to the market street, Nineveh humbled itself. God saw what they did and showed mercy.

lines.speaker: Throne
lines.text: Carved cedar, polished ivory, and the seat of a man who cannot command heaven.
